name: Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail
name_original: Rambla de las Hortichuelas y Playa de los Bajos – carrera desde Aguadulce
description: Jog the easy 2.7-mile Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail for coastal views, historical ruins, and Posidonia meadows.
region_intro: Jogging around Roquetas De Mar offers diverse routes along its extensive coastline and through natural areas. The region is characterized by long promenades, sandy beaches, and coastal paths like the Puerta Verde, providing mostly flat terrain ideal for running. Inland, the landscape includes natural parks with sand dunes and salt flats, alongside urban green spaces. These features combine to create varied running environments, from scenic seaside stretches to tranquil park trails. Best jogging routes around Roquetas De Mar The most popular jogging route is Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete. This flat coastal path offers views of historical ruins and Posidonia meadows. Another top favourite among local runners is Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop from Roquetas de Mar, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.0 km) path. This route features mostly paved terrain around historical Santa Ana Castle and along the beach. Local runners also love the Las Palmerillas Beach – Start of the Corredor Verde loop from Campillo del Moro, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through coastal areas and green spaces, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Jogging around Roquetas De Mar is defined by long promenades, sandy beaches, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal runs to moderate loops. The routes in Roquetas De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore Roquetas De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Roquetas de Mar?
A: Jogging routes in Roquetas de Mar primarily feature flat terrain, especially along the extensive promenade and coastal paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986588111/puerta-verde-de-roquetas-de-mar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail&lt;/a&gt;. You'll find a mix of paved surfaces, sandy beaches, and natural park landscapes with sand dunes and salt flats, offering varied environments for your run.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Roquetas de Mar offers several easy routes perfect for beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986588111/puerta-verde-de-roquetas-de-mar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, being a flat coastal path. Overall, 17 of the available routes are classified as easy, providing gentle options for those new to running or looking for a relaxed pace.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Roquetas de Mar?
A: Many running routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore the diverse ecosystems of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1501230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, including sand dunes and salt flats like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Wetlands of Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also visible on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Roquetas de Mar?
A: Roquetas de Mar boasts over 320 clear days annually with minimal rainfall, making it an ideal destination for year-round outdoor activities. The pleasant climate ensures comfortable running conditions throughout most of the year, though early mornings or late evenings are recommended during the peak summer months to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes suitable for all ages?
A: Yes, the flat and well-maintained promenades and coastal paths are generally suitable for families. Urban green spaces like Maria Cano Park also offer pleasant, safe environments for a family run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986588111/puerta-verde-de-roquetas-de-mar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail&lt;/a&gt;, with its easy grade and coastal views, is a great option for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Roquetas de Mar?
A: While many outdoor areas in Roquetas de Mar are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails, especially within natural parks like Punta Entinas-Sabinar. Generally, dogs on a leash are welcome on promenades and many coastal paths, but always ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Roquetas de Mar are designed as loops, offering a convenient circular experience. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21837571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Palmerillas Beach – Start of the Corredor Verde loop&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Roquetas de Mar?
A: The running routes in Roquetas de Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic seaside stretches to tranquil park trails, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there options for longer or more challenging runs?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more challenging run, there are 26 routes classified as difficult and 59 as moderate. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 6.9 miles (11.0 km), offers a moderate challenge with mostly paved terrain and takes about 1 hour 10 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Yes, while primarily coastal, some routes or nearby areas feature water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4103980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de la Chanata&lt;/a&gt; is a notable lake highlight in the region. Additionally, the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park includes salt flats and wetlands, offering unique water-related landscapes for exploration.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in Roquetas de Mar?
A: There are over 100 dedicated jogging routes available around Roquetas de Mar, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit every runner. This includes 17 easy routes, 59 moderate routes, and 26 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of historical sites?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986588111/puerta-verde-de-roquetas-de-mar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers glimpses of historical ruins along its coastal path.
tips: Aesthetic abandoned building that can be seen from Playa de los Bajos or the path that goes inside.

The Roquetas de Mar Green Gateway is a coastal path that follows part of the old Cañada Real de la Costa (Royal Coastal Cattle Track), connecting Roquetas de Mar with Aguadulce. It's a flat, short route (between 1.7 and 1.9 km depending on the source) suitable for hikers and cyclists.
In the final section, there are several wooden bridges that allow you to cross areas that may occasionally flood.
A favorite place for runners and cyclists, it connects Roquetas with Aguadulce, along the same beach

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail is considered easy with a "Baja" (Low) difficulty level. It is completely flat with no significant elevation gain, making it suitable for all fitness levels and an easy, tranquil jogging experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of asphalt and pressed gravel sections. You'll also encounter sandy and saline soils, and wooden bridges in areas that can occasionally be subject to flooding. Dedicated zones are available for both pedestrians and cyclists.

faqs.question: What natural features and historical sites can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the Mediterranean Sea, pass through scrubland with palm trees, and skirt the protected area of Ribera de la Algaida. A wooden viewpoint offers a chance to observe the natural monument of Posidonia oceanica meadows. Historically, you'll pass the ruins of the Old Carabineros Barracks and cross Wetlands Bridges. The trail also leads to Rambla de las Hortichuelas Beach.

faqs.question: Are there good spots for sunset views?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. This coastal trail is an ideal spot to enjoy beautiful sunsets over the Mediterranean Sea, especially as it connects Roquetas de Mar with Aguadulce.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a popular greenway connecting Roquetas de Mar and Aguadulce, you can typically find street parking or public parking lots near the coastal access points in either town. Look for areas close to the promenade or beach access points.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options, such as local bus services, are available in Roquetas de Mar and Aguadulce. Check local bus routes that serve the coastal areas of these towns, as the trail runs along the coast and is easily accessible from various points.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail is primarily an out-and-back route, connecting Roquetas de Mar with Aguadulce along the coast. It is not a circular loop.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given its popularity with joggers and cyclists, and its proximity to protected natural areas. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the natural monument it passes through?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to access the Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar Trail. While it traverses the Monumento Natural Arrecife Barrera de Posidonia, access to the trail itself is free and open to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for jogging?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended during those months. Winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with sections of the "Puerta Verde de Roquetas de Mar" (another segment of the greenway), and the "Cycle Path Paseo Maritimo Playa Las Salinas".
